headline: Backbench MPs speak average 4.2 minutes per parliamentary session
context: Analysis of Hansard records showed backbench MPs spoke for an average of only 4.2 minutes per parliamentary session in 2025, with some not speaking at all.

cards.big: Backbenchers average 4.2 minutes of speaking time per session — 47 spoke zero minutes
cards.sub: Most MPs are silent observers in the institution meant to amplify their voice

cards.big: Standing Order 35 limits debate but gives Speaker discretion on time allocation
cards.sub: Speaker's discretion typically favours front bench over backbench contributions

cards.big: Each backbencher costs constituents RM1.2 million annually in salary and allowances
cards.sub: 4.2 minutes of parliamentary speaking costs RM285,714 per minute per MP

cards.big: New Zealand allocates guaranteed minimum speaking time to every MP per session
cards.sub: Parliamentary systems can ensure all elected representatives have meaningful voice

cards.big: An MP who never speaks in Parliament is a RM1.2 million annual investment in silence
Should every MP be guaranteed minimum speaking time proportional to their constituency size?
